noise on Image A

chromatic abberation on Image A
warmer WB on Image A
artifacts around edges on Image B

Highlights (mainly the sky) are better on the Image A, with no overexposure areas and more detail. The Image B has significant overexposure areas and little detail in the sky.
Midtones and shadows are brighter on the Image B, while the Image A has them noticeably darker.
Generally, the level of detail and structure is similar on both, but in a few places the structure is better on the Image B.
Significantly more noise on the Image A (especially in midtones, shadows, and edges).
Visible and higher local contrast on the Image B, too high; the Image A looks better here.
Chromatic aberration on the Image A

Wider tonal range on the Image A, less overexposure (especially in the sky)
Slightly brighter shadows on the Image A
Higher contrast on the Image B, too high
Shadows lean too much into the black tones, resulting in slightly less detail (related to contrast) on Image B
Lower saturation on the Image B
Higher noise on the Image A, especially in shadows
Slightly visible color spots on the Image B
Jagged edges with strange shadows on the Image B
Slightly jagged edges also on the Image A, but less visible due to the lower contrast
Slightly visible chromatic aberration on the Image A
Stronger sharpening in midtones on the Image B

brighter sky on Image B
overexposure on highlights on Image B
brighter midtones on Image B
higher contrast on Image B
higher noise on Image A

Wider tonal range on the Image A, especially visible in the sky, which is not overexposed.
Brighter shadows on the character on the Image A.
Brighter background on the Image B.
Brighter face on the Image A.
More skin detail (scars, pores, and eyebrows are more visible) on the Image A.
More detail on the character (which is in the foreground) on the Image A than on the Image B, but the situation is reversed in the background, with the Image B having more detail (background blur is weaker).
Higher saturation of red colors on the skin of the face on the Image B.
Slight noise is visible on the subject on the Image A, which is stronger than on the Image B.
